title: Port of LA/Long Beach congestion — supply chain crisis
description: The Port of LA/Long Beach — handling 40% of US containerized imports — becomes severely congested, with up to 100 ships anchored offshore awaiting berths. Wait times reach 3–4 weeks. Container spot rates hit $20,000+ per 40ft box. Retailers charter private vessels and divert to East Coast ports. Biden administration announces 24/7 operations on October 13, 2021, but congestion persists through the holiday season.
source: Marine Exchange of Southern California, Freightos
